{{Short description|2005 Italian film festival}} {{Infobox Film festival |name = 62nd Venice International Film Festival |image = VeniceFilmFestival62poster.jpg |image_alt = |image_size = 250px |caption = Festival poster |opening = ''[[Seven Swords]]'' |closing = ''[[Perhaps Love (2005 film)|Perhaps Love]]'' |location = [[Venice]], [[Italy]] |founded = 1932 |awards = [[Golden Lion]]:
''[[Brokeback Mountain]]'' |artistic_director= Marco Müller |host = [[Inés Sastre]] |number = |date = 31 August – 10 September 2005 |website = [http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/ Website] | chronology = Venice Film Festival | preceded_by = [[61st Venice International Film Festival|61st]] | followed_by = [[63rd Venice International Film Festival|63rd]] }} The '''62nd annual [[Venice Film Festival|Venice International Film Festival]]''', was held from 31 August 2005 to 10 September 2005, at [[Venice Lido]] in [[Italy]].{{cite web |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/exhibition/calendar/en/17492.html |title=62nd Venice Film Festival - August 31, 2005 |work=labiennale.org |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20051219090259/http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/exhibition/calendar/en/17492.html |archive-date=2005-12-19 |access-date=2018-05-27}} Italian production and costume designer [[Dante Ferretti]] was the jury president for the main competition. Spanish actress [[Inés Sastre]] hosted the opening and closing nights of the festival.{{cite web |date=19 April 2010 |title=The 2000s |url=http://www.carnivalofvenice.com/?page_id=503&lang=en |access-date=October 8, 2013}}{{cite web |title=VENICE 2005 – THE 62nd MOSTRA DEL CINEMA |url=http://www.americancinemapapers.com/files/VENICE_2005.htm |access-date=October 6, 2013}} The [[Golden Lion]] was awarded to [[Ang Lee]]'s ''[[Brokeback Mountain]]''.{{cite web |title=62nd Venice Film Festival - Golden Globe Awards for Ang Lee's Brokeback Mountain |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/news/cinema/en/61511.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060207091632/http://www.labiennale.org/en/news/cinema/en/61511.html |archive-date=2006-02-07 |access-date=2018-05-27 |work=labiennale.org}} Japanese filmmaker [[Hayao Miyazaki]] and Italian actress [[Stefania Sandrelli]] the recipients of the [[Golden Lion for Lifetime Achievement]].{{cite web |title=62nd Venice Film Festival - Hayao Miyazaki Golden Lion for Lifetime Achievement |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/news/cinema/en/16959.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20051219084526/http://www.labiennale.org/en/news/cinema/en/16959.html |archive-date=2005-12-19 |access-date=2018-05-27 |work=labiennale.org}}{{cite web |title=62nd Venice Film Festival - Stefania Sandrelli Golden Lion for Lifetime Achievement |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/news/cinema/en/18200.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20051228214234/http://www.labiennale.org/en/news/cinema/en/18200.html |archive-date=2005-12-28 |access-date=2018-05-27 |work=labiennale.org}} [[Digital film]]s competed in all categories for the first time in the festival's history. During this edition of the festival, an International Design Competition of the new Palazzo del Cinema took place. The winner of the competition was ''5+1 & Rudy Ricciotti''. The purpose of the new building is to house the main headquarters of the Film Festival, as well as congresses and cultural events.{{cite web|url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/news/cinema/en/21892.html |title=62nd Venice Film Festival - International Design Competition of the new Palazzo del Cinema |work=labiennale.org |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060516031813/http://www.labiennale.org/en/news/cinema/en/21892.html |archive-date=16 May 2006 |df=dmy}} The festival opened with ''[[Seven Swords]]'' by [[Tsui Hark]], and closed with ''[[Perhaps Love (2005 film)|Perhaps Love]]'' by [[Peter Chan]]. ==Juries== === Main Competition (''Venezia 62'') === * [[Dante Ferretti]], Italian production designer, art director and costume designer - Jury President{{cite web |title=62nd Venice Film Festival - The international juries of the 62nd Mostra |url=http://www.labiennale.org:80/en/news/cinema/en/17425.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20051025105144/http://www.labiennale.org/en/news/cinema/en/17425.html |archive-date=2005-10-25 |access-date=2018-09-26 |work=labiennale.org}}{{cite web |title=Juries for the 2000s |url=http://www.carnivalofvenice.com/?page_id=527&lang=en |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170804133752/http://www.carnivalofvenice.com/?page_id=527&lang=en |archive-date=August 4, 2017 |access-date=October 8, 2013}} * [[Ah Cheng]], Chinese author and screenwriter * [[Claire Denis]], French filmmaker * [[Amos Gitai]], Israeli filmmaker * [[Edgar Reitz]], German filmmaker and Professor of Film * [[Emilíana Torrini]], Icelandic singer, songwriter and actress * [[Christine Vachon]], American producer active in independent film sector === Horizons (''Orizzonti'') === * [[Mimmo Rotella]], Italian artist, closely related to cinema - Jury President * [[Isabel Coixet]], Spanish filmmaker * [[Jean-Michel Frodon]], French film critic * [[Valerio Mastandrea]], Italian actor * [[Shinya Tsukamoto]], Japanese director and actor === Short Film Competition (''Corto Cortissimo'') === * Chema Prado, Spanish film critic - Jury President * [[Giovanna Gagliardo]], Italian filmmaker * Clemens Klopfenstein, Swiss film director === ''Opera Prima'' ("Luigi de Laurentiis" Award for a Debut Film) === In a contest that examines all feature-length films that are first works present in the various sections of the Festival, this jury assigns the "Lion of the Future - Luigi De Laurentis award for best debut work" to one film, as well as a prize of Euro 100,000 put forward by [[Filmauro]] and of 20,000 metres of film stock offered by [[Kodak]]. * [[Guy Maddin]], Canadian screenwriter, director, author, cinematographer and editor - Jury President * [[Peter Cowie]], British film historian and author * [[Isabella Ferrari]], Italian actress * [[Ismaël Ferroukhi]], French-Moroccan film director * [[Renata Litvinova]], Russian actress, director and screenwriter ==Official Sections== ===In competition=== The following films were selected for the main competition:{{cite web |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/exhibition/program/en/14372.html |title=62nd Venice Film Festival - |柳生一族の陰謀 |1978 |} ===Retrospective - The Secret History of Italian Cinema 2=== A retrospective section on [[Cinema of Italy|Italian film]] (1946 to 1976). This section is part of a planned 4-year retrospective on some lesser known sides of Italian Cinema that started on the 61st edition of the festival.{{cite web |url=http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/exhibition/program/en/14362.html |title=62nd Venice Film Festival |work=labiennale.org |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20060109080104/http://www.labiennale.org/en/cinema/exhibition/program/en/14362.html |archive-date=2006-01-09 |access-date=2018-05-27 }} {| class="wikitable" style="width:95%; margin-bottom:0px" |- !
